ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for grilled chicken teriyaki; FOODON_03302050 (chicken dish) used as the closest defensible broad match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (chicken meat, grilled) used as the closest defensible match; teriyaki sauce component not separately coded. No exact FoodEx2 code exists for grilled chicken teriyaki as a composite dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for grilled chicken teriyaki as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of grilled chicken teriyaki is approximately 150g, equivalent to one medium chicken thigh or breast fillet with teriyaki glaze, as commonly served in a Japanese restaurant or hawker stall in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — 804D Grilled Chicken Teriyaki
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for item 804D Grilled Chicken Teriyaki. All per_100g values are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and fatty acid breakdown estimated from USDA FoodData Central (NDB 05064, chicken thigh, grilled) as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for grilled chicken teriyaki as a composite dish. Chicken meat itself has a negligible GI due to near-zero carbohydrate content. The teriyaki sauce contributes a small carbohydrate load (2.36g/100g total). A GI of 50 (low) is estimated based on the analogous mixed protein-sauce dish category from the University of Sydney GI Database, reflecting the dominant protein matrix with minor sugar contribution from teriyaki glaze. Glycemic load per 150g serving = (50 × 2.36 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.77, rounded to 1.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 450mg/100g — high sodium from soy-based teriyaki sauce
hypertension.guidance: At 450mg sodium per 100g, a 150g serving delivers approximately 675mg sodium — over 30% of the 2000mg daily limit recommended for hypertension management; patients should request reduced-sodium soy sauce preparation and limit portion size, pairing with potassium-rich vegetables such as spinach or sweet potato to partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (2.36g/100g) and high protein (22.31g/100g) support glycaemic stability
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 2.36g carbohydrate per 100g and a high protein content of 22.31g, grilled chicken teriyaki has a minimal glycaemic impact and is suitable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes when paired with low-GI vegetables rather than white rice; however, teriyaki sauce sugar content can vary by recipe and patients should confirm with the vendor.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (450mg/100g) is the primary cardiovascular concern; saturated fat is moderate at ~1.6g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat content of approximately 1.6g/100g is well within cardiovascular-safe limits, and grilling avoids added cooking fats; however, the high sodium content of 450mg/100g poses a cardiovascular risk and patients with CVD should request reduced-sodium preparation and limit serving size to 100g or less.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sodium from soy sauce may worsen RA-related tissue swelling; grilling method and low saturated fat are favourable
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Grilled preparation avoids pro-inflammatory trans fats and the saturated fat content (~1.6g/100g) is well below the 5g/100g RA threshold; however, the 450mg/100g sodium load from soy-based teriyaki sauce can exacerbate RA-related oedema and patients should request reduced-sodium preparation and ensure chicken skin is removed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Chicken Teriyaki (鶏の照り焼き, Tori no Teriyaki) is a Japanese dish consisting of chicken — typically thigh or breast — marinated and grilled with a teriyaki glaze made from soy sauce, mirin, sake, and sugar. The cooking method produces a caramelised, glossy exterior with a savoury-sweet flavour profile and a tender, juicy interior. In Singapore, this dish is commonly served in Japanese restaurants and hawker stalls as item 804D. Per 100g, it provides 151 kcal, 22.31g protein, 5.81g fat, and only 2.36g carbohydrate, making it a high-protein, low-carbohydrate option. The sodium content of 450mg/100g is elevated due to the soy-based marinade and is the primary clinical concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients. The low carbohydrate content and high protein make it suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management when paired with low-GI sides. Saturated fat is approximately 1.6g/100g, well within cardiovascular-safe limits. The dish is dairy-free but contains soy and gluten allergens from the teriyaki sauce. It is appropriate for lunch and dinner meal contexts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chicken teriyaki?
Is chicken teriyaki good for diabetics?
Is teriyaki chicken high in sodium?
Can I eat chicken teriyaki if I have high blood pressure?
How much protein in grilled chicken teriyaki?
Is 804D chicken teriyaki suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (item 804D, confidence 0.95). Fatty acid breakdown estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest match (grilled chicken thigh) and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework, appropriately documented.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.62g/100g), monounsaturated_fat_g (2.19g/100g), and polyunsaturated_fat_g (1.27g/100g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 05064 (chicken thigh, cooked, roasted) as HPB Foodo does not report fatty acid breakdown for this item; values are proportionally consistent with the total fat of 5.81g/100g reported by HPB. Trans fat is reported as 0.0g based on grilled preparation without hydrogenated fats. 2. Sugar is reported as 0.0g/100g by HPB Foodo; confirmed zero per the HPB source, though teriyaki sauce recipes vary and actual sugar content may differ by vendor preparation. 3. Dietary fibre is reported as 0.0g/100g by HPB Foodo; confirmed zero for this preparation as no vegetable components are included in the base dish. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for item 804D Grilled Chicken Teriyaki, and no supplementary approved source was used to estimate them for this dish-level record. 5. GI of 50 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for grilled chicken teriyaki as a composite dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. The dominant protein matrix suppresses glycaemic response and the low carbohydrate content (2.36g/100g) results in a very low glycaemic load of 1.8 per 150g serving. 6. Halal status is marked false as this cannot be confirmed without vendor certification; the dish may be halal-certified at specific outlets. 7. FoodEx2 match type corrected from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' as 'adapted' is not a valid value in the allowed set ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none']; A0CKP (chicken meat, grilled) remains the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor with the teriyaki sauce component undocumented in FoodEx2.
